scenario: A BPMN element has cryptic ID Activity_0xyz123. Team wants to add inline description / documentation explaining purpose.
question: BPMN element documentation field?

options.text: 0 specification defines a <bpmn:documentation> element that can be added as a child of any FlowElement, including Service Tasks, User Tasks, Subprocesses, Events, Gateways, and Sequence Flows. This element contains free-form text describing the element's purpose, variable contracts, business rules, or operational notes. Camunda 8's Web Modeler and Desktop Modeler display this text in the element's property panel, making it directly accessible to modelers. The documentation has no runtime effect but is invaluable for maintainability and team communication when working with complex diagrams.

0 element that accepts free-form text or HTML (with optional textFormat attribute). It can be added to Service Tasks, User Tasks, Events, Gateways, Subprocesses, and Sequence Flows. In Camunda 8, Web Modeler shows the documentation in the property panel, and Desktop Modeler does the same. Multiple documentation children are allowed per element. The documentation has no effect on process execution; it is purely metadata. For a cryptic ID like Activity_0xyz123, adding documentation explaining the element's purpose, required input variables, and expected outputs greatly improves diagram readability.

Only XML comments -- this is only partially correct. -- -->) can be added to BPMN XML files, they are not part of the BPMN specification's documentation model and are typically stripped by BPMN tools. The proper BPMN mechanism is the dedicated <bpmn:documentation> element, which is preserved by tools, displayed in modeler property panels, and exported with the diagram. XML comments are invisible to process engineers using the visual modeler interface. Relying only on XML-level comments would require users to open the raw XML to read the documentation, which is impractical for business process modeling workflows.

Custom extension -- this overstates the complexity. 0 specification defined in the base schema. It does not require any <bpmn:extensionElements> wrapper or Camunda-specific namespacing. Any BPMN-compliant tool should read and preserve this element. Treating documentation as a custom extension would suggest it requires special handling or custom parsing, when in fact it is a first-class citizen of the BPMN model accessible through the standard property panel in all Camunda modelers.

a.text: Incorrect. BPMN documentation element: Spec definition: `xml Sends an order confirmation email to customer. Requires: customerEmail variable. Returns: emailMessageId in process scope. ... Visible in modelers: Web Modeler: Property panel: documentation field. Edit inline. Desktop Modeler: Similar property panel. Markdown support varies. Engine impact: None at runtime: documentation is metadata. Use cases: Use case 1: Element purpose: "This task validates VAT against EU VIES service." Explains why this exists. Use case 2: Variable contracts: "Input: customerId. Output: customer.tier set." Documents data flow. Use case 3: Special considerations: "Idempotent; safe to retry." "Long-running; activation timeout 5 min." Use case 4: Business rules: "Skip for amounts Primary description. Detailed HTML description with links ]]> Per spec, multiple allowed with optional textFormat. textFormat attribute (verify support): text/plain (default). text/html. text/markdown. Auto-generated docs: Tooling: extract documentation; generate process docs. Web Modeler: may show docs in collaboration views. Audit: documentation visible in Operate (verify). For element types: Activities: tasks, subprocesses. Events: starts, intermediates, ends. Gateways: decision logic. Sequence flows: optional; describe condition purpose. For process header: xml End-to-end order process from creation to fulfillment. Owner: order-team@example.com SLA: 24 hours ... ` Process-level docs: ownership, SLA, etc. Best practices: Document non-obvious: not what's already in name. Why, not what: code (XML) shows what; docs explain why. Variable contracts: inputs/outputs per task. Owner / contact: who maintains. Update with changes: keep docs current. Anti-patterns: No

b.text: Correct. Option c) -- Partial. XML comments don't appear in property panel.

explanation: Option c) -- Partial. XML comments don't appear in property panel.

findings.message: option text contains its own verdict/rationale
